retrieved_docs.title: Salpingitis Isthmica Nodosa -- Introduction -- Anatomy
retrieved_docs.content: Fallopian tubes typically range from 10 to 14 cm in length and have an external diameter of approximately 1 cm. [3] They are described as having 4 different parts: the fimbriae, infundibulum, ampulla, and isthmus, which connect the fallopian tubes to the uterus. [4] The lumen at the isthmus is relatively small (1-2 mm) and is surrounded by a 3-layered muscular wall. This consists of a middle circular layer sandwiched between inner and outer longitudinal layers. [5] Salpingitis isthmica nodosa causes nodular swelling (up to a few centimeters in diameter) predominantly at the isthmus; however, it can involve all other portions of the fallopian tube. [6] The outer serosa of the nodules are smooth and yellow, grey, or brown in color. [7]

retrieved_docs.title: Salpingitis Isthmica Nodosa -- Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: Laparoscopy of a patient with salpingitis isthmica nodosa will inevitably identify nodular swelling and thickening of the isthmus. [21] This is caused by the hypertrophy and hyperplasia of the mesosalpinx around the diverticula pouches. However, diagnosis can only be made following histological assessment. Simultaneous chromopertubation during laparoscopy will identify the diverticular defects along the tube. In severe cases, the anatomy of the tubes will appear grossly abnormal. However, with diluted methylene blue, subtle repetitive notching within the fallopian tube is seen underneath the serosal layer.

retrieved_docs.title: Minimally Invasive Glaucoma Surgery -- Technique or Treatment -- iStent Inject
retrieved_docs.content: The device is available as a pre-loaded injector (G2-M-IS injector system) with two stents placed nasally in the TM and Schlemm canal 30 to 60 degrees apart. Fea et al., in their analysis, documented a reduction in IOP of 35 to 39% with a decrease in medication load, and 65 to 75% of patients were free of medications at one year. One of the previous studies showed that the IOP reduction by implantation of two stents is equivalent to IOP reduction with two antiglaucoma drugs after one year. [8]

retrieved_docs.title: Dermatopathology Evaluation of Cysts -- Anatomical Pathology -- Pilar cysts (ie, trichilemmal cysts, isthmus-catagen cysts, sebaceous cysts)
retrieved_docs.content: Microscopic features of pilar cysts can include: Unilocular (occasionally multilocular), without connection to the epidermis (ie, no punctum) Stratified squamous epithelium lining with trichilemmal keratinization (with keratin 10/17 expression) Cells with basaloid nuclei that become larger with abundant pale cytoplasm towards the cyst lumen and keratinize to eosinophilic compact keratin without an intervening granular layer (see Image. Pilar Cyst) Calcification and cholesterol clefts in the cyst contents (see Image. Pilar Cyst Contents).

retrieved_docs.title: Dermatopathology Evaluation of Cysts -- Anatomical Pathology -- Pilar cysts (ie, trichilemmal cysts, isthmus-catagen cysts, sebaceous cysts)
retrieved_docs.content: Pilar cysts originate from the external (outer) root sheath at the level of the follicular isthmus. The cysts usually present as a solitary nodule most commonly located on the scalp of a female adult. [19] During treatment, cysts can be easily removed with the cyst lining intact, giving a shelled-out appearance. Cystic trichilemmal carcinoma is an in-situ carcinoma that may be found within a pilar cyst (see Image. Trichilemmal Carcinoma). [20] [21]

retrieved_docs.title: Neurothekeoma -- Etiology
retrieved_docs.content: There has been considerable confusion in characterizing and defining the origin of neurothekeomas. In 1980, Gallager and Helwig first classified neurothekeomas as dermal tumors of neural origin with characteristic clinical features and distinctive histology. Clinical features included a flesh-colored or slightly erythematous nodule of soft consistency located on the head, upper extremities, or trunk and arising more commonly in young females. Histologic features included spindled cells arranged in nests and cords with a mucinous, myxoid background containing dermal collagen bundles. Because the tumor cells appear similar to Schwann cells on electron microscopy (spindle-shaped and surrounded by a basement membrane without myofilament or melanosomes), the authors proposed "neurothekeoma" from the Greek word for sheath. [7] In 1986, Rosati et al reported cases of cellular neurothekeoma, believing them to be a cellular variant of the previously reported myxoid neurothekeoma. [8] In 1990, Barnhill and Mihm reported on several cases of cellular neurothekeoma, remarking on their histologic similarity to melanocytic tumors as well as commenting on their failure to react with S100 protein immunostain, unlike the original reported myxoid variant of neurothekeoma. Barnhill and Mihm suggested that cellular neurothekeoma represented a distinct subcategory of neurothekeoma and then, in 1991, suggested that cellular neurothekeoma might present an entirely separate entity. [1] [9] It is recognized now that at least some of the cases reported by Gallager and Helwig in 1980, as well as some subsequent reports of neurothekeoma, represented dermal nerve sheath myxoma, an entity of peripheral nerve sheath derivation that shares overlapping clinical and histologic features with neurothekeoma. [3] [4] [7] [4] [10] In 2005, Fetsch et al reported on a series of nerve sheath myxomas, the corresponding characteristic immunoprofile, and the tendency of nerve sheath myxoma to arise in different locations and exhibit a higher recurrence rate than neurothekeoma. [11] In 2007, 2 large case studies of neurothekeomas were published separately by Fetsch et al and Hornick and Fletcher, detailing the immunoprofile of neurothekeoma, further defining the entity as distinctive and separate from nerve sheath myxoma. [3] [4] Rather than displaying nerve sheath differentiation, it has been postulated that neurothekeoma is fibrohistiocytic. [3] [4] [5] [6]

retrieved_docs.title: Salpingitis Isthmica Nodosa -- Histopathology
retrieved_docs.content: Often, surrounding fibrous tissue or hypertrophy of smooth muscle is present, and endometrial-type stroma may be noted around the glands. The stromal response is typically minimal, and no significant atypia is usually present. These changes commonly occur in the proximal two-thirds of the fallopian tube. Majmudar suggested a histological classification system grade 1 to 3 based on the depth of the lumen within the myosalpinx. [1]

retrieved_docs.title: Histology, Thyroid Gland -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: The lobes are conical in shape and have an apex, a base, three surfaces – lateral, medial, and posterolateral, and two borders – the anterior and posterior. The isthmus, however, has two surfaces – anterior and posterior and two borders – superior and inferior.

retrieved_docs.title: Hysterosalpingogram -- Anatomy and Physiology
retrieved_docs.content: The anatomical structures primarily assessed during an HSG are the fallopian tubes and the endometrial cavity. In patients with normal anatomic reproductive structures, the fallopian tubes are patent and attach to the uterus at the interstitial space. During ovulation, the oocytes released from the ovaries pass through the patent fallopian tube lumens into the endometrial cavity. Each fallopian tube is on either side of the uterus, with the fimbriated end located near the ovary distally and the proximal end attached to the uterine cornua. The fallopian tubes are usually divided into 3 main segments: the infundibulum, ampulla, and isthmus.

retrieved_docs.title: Salpingitis Isthmica Nodosa -- Etiology
retrieved_docs.content: Though this condition was first described long ago in 1887 by Chiari, the underlying etiology of salpingitis isthmica nodosa frequently remains unknown. Currently, 3 primary etiologies have been proposed: infection, cellular invasion, and congenital malformations. [8]

retrieved_docs.title: Embryology, Thyroid -- Pathophysiology
retrieved_docs.content: Absent Isthmus - A normal thyroid isthmus is commonly located at the second and third tracheal rings, but can vary from the cricoid cartilage down to the fourth tracheal ring. [5] In rare cases, the isthmus is absent. While the right and left lobes are separated, they are often functional. [9]

retrieved_docs.title: May-Thurner Syndrome -- Histopathology
retrieved_docs.content: The three histologic types of venous spurs in May-Thurner syndrome are central, lateral, and fenestrated. [8]

retrieved_docs.title: Dermatopathology Evaluation of Cysts -- Anatomical Pathology -- Cystic panfolliculoma
retrieved_docs.content: Cystic panfolliculoma is an uncommon, benign tumor on the head in older adults that recapitulates multiple parts of the hair follicle (eg, infundibulum, isthmus, matrix). [35] [36] Histopathology reveals a well-circumscribed cyst with foci of differentiation from multiple parts of the hair follicle (eg, thin infundibular epithelium with foci of matrical or outer root sheath differentiation); BerEP4 can be used to label the follicular germinative differentiation peripherally, while Cytokeratin 903 and Cytokeratin 5/6 can stain the tumor cells. [37]

retrieved_docs.title: Atrioventricular Block -- Evaluation -- Classification of Atrioventricular Block
retrieved_docs.content: Second degree, Mobitz type I (Wenckebach). AV block is characterized by P waves exhibiting a consistent rate (<100 bpm) and a gradual prolongation of the PR interval. This is followed by periodic occurrences of single non-conducted P waves, typically accompanied by P waves preceding and following the non-conducted P waves. (Figure.2) Wenckebach is a phenomenon commonly associated with the AV nodes; however, there are reported cases of Wenckebach associated with infra-nodal AV block. [34]

retrieved_docs.title: Histology, Thyroid Gland -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: It is a gland consisting of two lobes, the right, and the left lobes, joined together by an intermediate structure, the isthmus. Sometimes a third lobe called the pyramidal lobe projects from the isthmus. It has a fibrous/fibromuscular band, i.e., levator glandulae thyroideae running from the body of the hyoid to the isthmus. [1] The lobes are 5 x 2.5 x 2.5 cm in dimension and weigh around 25 gm. It extends from the fifth cervical to the first thoracic vertebrae. The lobes extend from the middle of the thyroid cartilage to the fifth tracheal ring. The isthmus is 1.2 x 1.2 cm in dimensions and extends from second to third tracheal rings. It grows larger in females during the period of menstruation and pregnancy.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 12 -- Level 3 -- Community 1923
retrieved_docs.content: The procedure “Closure of acquired urethrovaginal fistula (procedure)” is classified as a type of “Repair of urethral fistula,” indicating that it is a specific surgical approach within the broader category of urethral fistula repair. This closure procedure, as well as the “Closure of urethrovaginal fistula; with bulbocavernosus transplant,” are both linked to the pathology identified as “النَّاسُور,” reflecting a common underlying disease process. The “Repair of urethral fistula” itself is described as having the direct morphology “النَّاسُور,” reinforcing that the anatomical characteristic of the fistula is central to its management. Additionally, the “Repair of urethroperineal fistula” is noted as an inverse_isa relationship to the “Closure of acquired urethrovaginal fistula (procedure),” highlighting a close conceptual connection between these two repair techniques. A range of other fistula repair procedures—including “Repair of perineourethroscrotal fistula,” “Repair of rectovaginal fistula,” “Repair of sigmoidovaginal fistula,” “Repair of urethroperineal fistula,” and “Repair of vaginoileal fistula”—are all described as having the direct morphology “النَّاسُور.” This consistent morphological descriptor across diverse anatomical sites underscores that these various surgical repairs target fistulas sharing the same structural pathology. Recognizing the shared pathology and morphology of “النَّاسُور” among these procedures aids clinicians in applying unified diagnostic criteria and therapeutic principles when managing fistulas of the urethra, perineum, rectovagina, sigmoid colon, and ileum.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 4 -- Level 1 -- Community 236
retrieved_docs.content: fistulas. Each of these repairs shares the morphology “النَّاسُور” and is considered a form of “علاج الناسور المهبلي” (vaginal fistula therapy); “Repair of urethrovaginal fistula” is explicitly a child of this therapeutic entry. Surgical construction methods, including artificial vagina creation, are classified under “other or therapeutic procedures; female organs” and are grouped with repairs such as colovaginal fistula closure. Pelvis repair is linked by an inverse‑isa relationship to “رأب الردبة,” indicating that it is a specific instance of that broader concept. Collectively, these relationships define a structured taxonomy of female pelvic and vaginal surgical interventions, linking anatomical approaches (abdominal, transanal, vaginal, transvesical), procedural hierarchies (parent‑child, inverse‑subtype), and the common fistulous morphology “النَّاسُور,” thereby providing clinicians with a comprehensive framework for diagnosing, planning, and executing therapeutic procedures for

The method_of relationship ties Surgical action to both عملية في الإحليل and Surgical repair and revision of anastomosis, showing that identical surgical techniques are applied across generic urethral procedures and specific anastomotic repairs. These interconnected classifications provide clinicians with a clear taxonomy for documenting, planning, and coordinating care for patients requiring urethral or perineal operative management.

By mapping “isa” and “part‑of” relationships across these entities, the framework enables clinicians to select appropriate surgical routes, understand procedural interconnections, and provide coordinated, anatomy‑driven care for patients with genital fistulas and related structural defects.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 8 -- Level 1 -- Community 225
retrieved_docs.content: and to العمليات الجراحية في المهبل due to its anatomical proximity to the vagina. Operation on the pelvic region of the trunk is defined as a subtype of operation on periurethral tissue, situating it within the same peri‑urethral surgical domain. Procedures that involve the urethra but extend beyond the perineum, such as cystourethroscopy with percutaneous nephrostomy tube insertion, are categorized under “procedures on urethra” (isa). This structured framework connects specific urethral and peri‑urethral interventions to their anatomical bases, groups them within broader perineal, pelvic, and vaginal surgical categories, and provides a clear hierarchical model for understanding and managing female urethral and perineal conditions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 29 -- Level 3 -- Community 2434
retrieved_docs.content: The closure of rectovaginal fistula by a transanal approach, the closure of rectovaginal fistula by a vaginal approach, the repair of a vaginocutaneous fistula, the repair of a vaginoileal fistula, and the repair of a vaginoperineal fistula all share the same direct morphology, which is described by the term “النَّاسُور.” This common morphological descriptor indicates that, despite differences in the specific type of fistula or the surgical route employed, each procedure addresses a similar anatomical or pathological feature identified as “النَّاسُور.” Recognizing this shared morphology can aid clinicians in understanding the underlying tissue characteristics involved, standardizing surgical planning, and anticipating comparable postoperative considerations across these varied fistula repair techniques.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 32 -- Level 3 -- Community 2060
retrieved_docs.content: Delivery finding is a clinical observation that can be classified as a type (isa) of “baby is coming out,” indicating a live birth, and also as a type (isa) of “ولادة جنين ميت” (dead‑fetus delivery). The condition “إجهاض فائت” (missed abortion) has a relationship with “ولادة جنين ميت,” reflecting that a missed abortion may be associated with or result in a dead‑fetus delivery. This integrated view highlights the spectrum of delivery outcomes—from live birth to non‑viable fetal delivery—and underscores the importance of accurately distinguishing these scenarios for appropriate patient management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 34 -- Level 2 -- Community 985
retrieved_docs.content: The category VAGINA: SURGICAL REPAIRS, CLOSURES AND RECONSTRUCTIONS functions as a hierarchical hub for operative interventions that treat fistulous disease of the female genital tract, identified by the pathology النَّاسُور. Within this category, procedures such as Closure of acquired urethrovaginal fistula, Repair of urethral fistula, Repair of urethroperineal fistula (which has an inverse‑isa relationship with the former), Closure of urethrovaginal fistula with bulbocavernosus transplant, Closure of rectovaginal fistula by transanal approach, Closure of rectovaginal fistula by vaginal approach, Goodal‑Power operation on vagina, Repair of colovaginal fistula, Repair of intestinovaginal fistula, Repair of rectovaginal fistula, Repair of sigmoidovaginal fistula, Repair of vaginocutaneous fistula, Repair of vaginoileal fistula, Repair of vaginoperineal fistula, Closure of fistula of female perineum, and the treatment علاج الناسور المهبلي are all linked by a direct morphology relationship to النَّاسُور, indicating that each procedure addresses a fistulous lesion. The term النَّاسُور is also directly associated with علاج الناسور المهبلي, establishing a unified therapeutic framework for vaginal fistula management. Additionally, Repair of urethrovaginal fistula is listed as a child operation within the علاج الناسور المهبلي framework, and male urethrocutaneous fistula repair is included in the same morphological network. This integrated network of procedures and terminology highlights a comprehensive, coordinated approach to diagnosing, classifying, and surgically managing vaginal and adjacent fistulas across multiple anatomical sites.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 23 -- Level 3 -- Community 1661
retrieved_docs.content: Closure of acquired urethrovaginal fistula (procedure) is a specialized form of Repair of urethral fistula, serving as a child concept under that broader repair category. It is linked to the pathology النَّاسُور (fistula) and shares an inverse isa relationship with Repair of urethroperineal fistula, indicating close procedural similarity. Multiple repair procedures—including Repair of intestinovaginal fistula, Repair of male urethrocutaneous fistula, Repair of perineourethroscrotal fistula, Repair of sigmoidovaginal fistula, Repair of urethroperineal fistula, Repair of urethroscrotal fistula, and Repair of urethrovaginal fistula—are each characterized by the direct morphology النَّاسُور, underscoring that they all address fistulous lesions. The morphology النَّاسُور is further connected to the treatment concept علاج الناسور المهبلي (treatment of vaginal fistula), which is also listed as a child of Repair of urethrovaginal fistula. Together, these relationships delineate a hierarchical and morphological framework for the classification and management of various urethral and vaginal fistulas.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 31 -- Level 3 -- Community 2347
retrieved_docs.content: The closure of rectovaginal fistula by transanal approach, the repair of rectovaginal fistula, the repair of sigmoidovaginal fistula, the repair of vaginocutaneous fistula, and the repair of vaginoperineal fistula all share the same direct morphology, which is described by the term “النَّاسُور.” This common morphological characteristic indicates that, despite involving different anatomical sites, these fistula‑related procedures address a similar structural feature. Recognizing that “النَّاسُور” underlies the pathology of each of these fistulas can aid clinicians in selecting appropriate surgical techniques and in anticipating comparable intra‑operative considerations across these varied but morphologically related conditions.

Pelvis repair is an inverse‑isa instance of رأب الردبة, further linking pelvic repair to the broader concept of pelvic floor support. retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 4 -- Level 2 -- Community 1161
retrieved_docs.content: Urethral surgical repairs, closures and reconstructions constitute a broad category that includes a variety of specific operative procedures. Within this category, Penile urethrorrhaphy, Perineal urethrorrhaphy, Repair of male urethrocutaneous fistula, Repair of perineourethroscrotal fistula, and Repair of urethroscrotal fistula are all recognized as child procedures, reflecting their role in restoring urethral continuity and function. The same overarching category also has two major sub‑domains: “علاج الإحليل” (urethral treatment) and “تثبيت الإحليل” (urethral fixation). “Colporrhaphy for repair of urethrocele” is classified as an inverse‑isa of “علاج الإحليل,” indicating that it is a specific type of urethral treatment aimed at correcting urethrocele. Likewise, “Urethrovaginal fixation” is an inverse‑isa of “تثبيت الإحليل,” denoting it as a particular urethral fixation technique. Together, these relationships illustrate a hierarchical organization in which general categories of urethral repair, treatment, and fixation encompass distinct surgical interventions, providing clinicians with a structured framework for selecting appropriate procedures to address various urethral pathologies.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 26 -- Level 2 -- Community 776
retrieved_docs.content: اضطراب السبيل البولي is presented as a broad category of urinary tract disorders that includes more specific conditions such as تدلّي الإحليل (urethral dilatation) and حصاة الإحليل (urethral stone). The presence of حصاة الإحليل represents a narrower manifestation within اضطراب السبيل البولي, indicating that urethral stones are a particular type of urinary tract pathology. Lithotomy is identified as a general surgical procedure for stone removal, and the specific technique described as Removal of calculus of urethra with incision is an inverse‑isa (subtype) of Lithotomy. This focused procedure is directly associated with حصاة الإحليل, as it is the operative method whose primary focus is the extraction of urethral calculi. Together, these relationships illustrate that management of اضطراب السبيل البولي may involve urethral dilatation for structural issues and lithotomy‑based interventions, such as Removal of calculus of urethra with incision, for treating urethral stones.

retrieved_docs.content: Abdominal pregnancy (حمل منتبذ) is clinically associated with laparotomy (شق البطن), because surgical entry into the abdominal cavity is frequently required for the diagnosis, evaluation, and definitive management of this rare ectopic gestation.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 2 -- Community 1053
retrieved_docs.content: Other conditions or status of the mother complicating pregnancy, childbirth, or the puerperium is a broad diagnostic category that includes COMPLICATIONS MAINLY RELATED TO PREGNANCY. Within this category, a specific manifestation is Spotting complicating pregnancy, unspecified trimester, which is further classified as Early, first or unspecified trimester hemorrhage. Recognizing that spotting in an unspecified trimester represents an early‑trimester hemorrhagic event and is considered a pregnancy‑related complication helps clinicians organize maternal conditions that affect pregnancy, delivery, or the puerperium and guides appropriate assessment and management.
